title: PM Workfront Read-Only Lenses And Disposition History
objective: Extend the PM workfront as a read-only triage surface by adding operational lens counts, row-level lens tags, and compact last-PM-disposition context while preserving the existing return_to_lead mutation as the only PM-clicked action on the route.
required_inputs: PM Lane 003 user-initiated return-to-lead disposition loop
Focused backend, build, typecheck, and Playwright validation evidence
constrained_outputs: PM workfront read-model top-level lenses for all, blocked, needs PM disposition, returned to lead, stale blockers, and unassigned rows
PM workfront row-level lens_tags computed from existing readiness, blocking issue, ownership, follow-up, and audit context
PM workfront row-level last_pm_decision context derived from existing audit log rows without exposing the full audit payload
Operations-web read-only lens controls and returned-to-lead disposition-history display
Focused tests proving stale blocker and returned-to-lead lens transitions, last-PM-disposition context, no mutation from advisory open, and the unchanged PM-clicked return_to_lead request

No new read endpoint, no new mutation endpoint, no SQL or schema migration, no service admission, no auth or ingress widening, no assignment mutation, no schedule mutation, and no autonomous AI business-state action
validation_steps: Run focused mutation-seam PM workfront read-model, PM issue disposition, and pipeline integration pytest slices with SEAM_STORE_BACKEND=memory
Run operations-web typecheck after build-generated Next types are available
Run focused Playwright browser smoke for the PM workfront route
Run packet JSON validation and git diff whitespace checks before publication
sql_permission_note: Packet 2026-05-15-pm-lane-004 enriches an existing read projection and reuses memory-focused validation. No SQL, schema migration, production data write, auth, ingress, or new mutation authority is authorized.
